businessModel.text: Confirm how the business creates value before interpreting its ratios.

quality.text: Use profitability, balance-sheet strength, cash conversion, valuation, and sector drivers together before raising conviction.

profitability.text: Profitability should be durable and supported by operating evidence rather than one period.

balanceSheet.text: Debt and liquidity determine how well the business can absorb a weak cycle.

cashFlow.text: Cash generation should support reported profit before earnings quality is treated as strong.

valuation.text: Valuation needs growth and quality support; a low multiple is not automatically cheap.

sectorDrivers.text: Sector-specific operating drivers can matter more than generic ratios.

catalysts.text: Treat only supplied filings or operating improvements as catalysts.

risks.text: Missing or weakening business evidence can cap upside even when price momentum improves.

chart.summary: The chart trend is rising; treat it as timing evidence rather than a guaranteed forecast.

trend.text: The rising price trend is the main timing clue, but it needs agreement from volume and underlying exposure, liquidity, tracking, and valuation.

company.summary: Company-style fundamentals are not directly applicable to this index; evaluate its mandate, underlying exposure, costs, tracking, liquidity, and concentration instead.

businessModel.text: Treat the index structure, mandate, benchmark, and underlying exposure as its business context.

quality.text: Judge portfolio construction, replication quality, governance, liquidity, and consistency with the stated mandate.

profitability.text: Issuer profitability is not directly applicable; inspect the quality and earnings exposure of the underlying holdings when that data is available.

balanceSheet.text: A company balance sheet is not directly applicable; use underlying credit quality, leverage exposure, and counterparty structure where relevant.

cashFlow.text: Company cash flow is not directly applicable; assess distributions, underlying cash generation, and fund flows only when supplied.

valuation.text: Use NAV, premium or discount, underlying portfolio valuation, and costs rather than a standalone company multiple.

catalysts.text: Rebalancing, flows, rate changes, tracking improvement, and changes in underlying holdings are relevant only when supplied.

quality.text: Judge portfolio construction, replication quality, governance, liquidity, and consistency with the stated mandate.

profitability.text: Issuer profitability is not directly applicable; inspect the quality and earnings exposure of the underlying holdings when that data is available.

balanceSheet.text: A company balance sheet is not directly applicable; use underlying credit quality, leverage exposure, and counterparty structure where relevant.

cashFlow.text: Company cash flow is not directly applicable; assess distributions, underlying cash generation, and fund flows only when supplied.

valuation.text: Use NAV, premium or discount, underlying portfolio valuation, and costs rather than a standalone company multiple.

sectorDrivers.text: Rates, index composition, sector weights, flows, and mandate-specific drivers can change future returns.

catalysts.text: Rebalancing, flows, rate changes, tracking improvement, and changes in underlying holdings are relevant only when supplied.

risks.text: Tracking error, liquidity, concentration, costs, and underlying exposure can weaken the case even when the chart improves.

dataGaps.text: Important evidence is missing, so confidence must remain limited until it is refreshed.

concentration.text: A large single-position weight magnifies company-specific mistakes.

sizing.text: Size new or additional capital from the risk budget and invalidation distance.

chart.summary: The chart trend is rising; treat it as timing evidence rather than a guaranteed forecast.

trend.text: The rising price trend is the main timing clue, but it needs agreement from volume and underlying exposure, liquidity, tracking, and valuation.

momentum.text: Momentum must confirm the broader trend before it deserves more weight.

volume.text: Volume should confirm that price movement has enough participation to be dependable.

volatility.text: Position size and invalidation distance should allow for normal volatility without accepting unlimited downside.

levels.text: Use the supplied support, resistance, and invalidation levels as review points, not precise predictions.

patterns.text: Any detected pattern requires location, volume, and follow-through confirmation.

company.summary: Company-style fundamentals are not directly applicable to this unknown; evaluate its mandate, underlying exposure, costs, tracking, liquidity, and concentration instead.

businessModel.text: Treat the unknown structure, mandate, benchmark, and underlying exposure as its business context.

quality.text: Judge portfolio construction, replication quality, governance, liquidity, and consistency with the stated mandate.

profitability.text: Issuer profitability is not directly applicable; inspect the quality and earnings exposure of the underlying holdings when that data is available.

balanceSheet.text: A company balance sheet is not directly applicable; use underlying credit quality, leverage exposure, and counterparty structure where relevant.

cashFlow.text: Company cash flow is not directly applicable; assess distributions, underlying cash generation, and fund flows only when supplied.

valuation.text: Use NAV, premium or discount, underlying portfolio valuation, and costs rather than a standalone company multiple.

sectorDrivers.text: Rates, index composition, sector weights, flows, and mandate-specific drivers can change future returns.

catalysts.text: Rebalancing, flows, rate changes, tracking improvement, and changes in underlying holdings are relevant only when supplied.

risks.text: Tracking error, liquidity, concentration, costs, and underlying exposure can weaken the case even when the chart improves.

concentration.text: A large single-position weight magnifies company-specific mistakes.
